
<li data-start="775" data-end="866">
<p data-start="777" data-end="866">A <strong data-start="779" data-end="805">placeholder first book</strong> with your branding so the storefront isn’t empty at launch</p>
</li>
</ul>

<ul data-start="19" data-end="613">
<li data-start="19" data-end="239">

<ul data-start="68" data-end="239">
<li data-start="68" data-end="149">
<p data-start="70" data-end="149"><strong data-start="70" data-end="100">Admin account (app-level):</strong> <code data-start="101" data-end="149">Chapterhouse_fields14_admin@chapterhouse.local</code></p>
</li>
<li data-start="152" data-end="239">
<p data-start="154" data-end="239"><strong data-start="154" data-end="182">First author (pen name):</strong> <code data-start="183" data-end="239">Chapterhouse_temonet-damone_author1@chapterhouse.local</code></p>
</li>
</ul>
</li>
<li data-start="240" data-end="348">
<p data-start="242" data-end="348">A <strong data-start="244" data-end="264">placeholder book</strong> (“Gate of Paper Cities”) credited to TéMonét Damone, so the storefront isn’t empty.</p>
</li>
<li data-start="349" data-end="556">
<p data-start="351" data-end="471">A secure, one-time <strong data-start="370" data-end="395">Auth seeding endpoint</strong> to actually create those two users in Supabase Auth using your service key:</p>
<ul data-start="474" data-end="556">
<li data-start="474" data-end="556">
<p data-start="476" data-end="556"><code data-start="476" data-end="504">POST /api/admin/seed-users</code> with header <code data-start="517" data-end="556">x-seed-token: YOUR_TEST_CLEANUP_TOKEN</code></p>
</li>
</ul>
</li>
<li data-start="557" data-end="613">
<p data-start="559" data-end="613">Updated <strong data-start="567" data-end="584">setup_all.sql</strong> and <strong data-start="589" data-end="599">README</strong> instructions.</p>
</li>
</ul>
<p data-start="615" data-end="766">📦 <strong data-start="618" data-end="651">Download the updated package:</strong><br data-start="651" data-end="654">
<strong data-start="654" data-end="766"><span class="" data-state="closed"><a data-start="656" data-end="764" class="cursor-pointer">chapterhouse-final-visual-with-accounts.zip</a></span></strong></p>
<h3 data-start="768" data-end="806">How to activate the accounts (SBS)</h3>
<ol data-start="807" data-end="1429">
<li data-start="807" data-end="865">
<p data-start="810" data-end="865">Supabase → SQL Editor → run <code data-start="838" data-end="862">supabase/setup_all.sql</code>.</p>
</li>
<li data-start="866" data-end="925">
<p data-start="869" data-end="925">Deploy or run locally with <code data-start="896" data-end="918">SUPABASE_SERVICE_KEY</code> set.</p>
</li>
<li data-start="926" data-end="1291">
<p data-start="929" data-end="960">Call the seeding endpoint once:</p>

(</span><span><span class="hljs-string">'host'</span></span><span>,</span><span><span class="hljs-string">'Mara'</span></span><span>,</span><span><span class="hljs-string">'Readers—drop a 1-to-5 on how easy it was to find a book today.'</span></span><span>);
</span></span></code></div></div></pre>
<h3 data-start="814" data-end="835">Where to put this</h3>
<ul data-start="836" data-end="985">
<li data-start="836" data-end="892">
<p data-start="838" data-end="892">Supabase → <strong data-start="849" data-end="863">SQL Editor</strong> → run after <code data-start="876" data-end="891">setup_all.sql</code>.</p>
</li>
<li data-start="893" data-end="985">
<p data-start="895" data-end="985">Or paste it at the bottom of <code data-start="924" data-end="948">supabase/setup_all.sql</code> in your repo, then re-run that file.</p>
</li>
</ul>
<h3 data-start="987" data-end="1003">Don’t forget</h3>
<ul data-start="1004" data-end="1134">
<li data-start="1004" data-end="1134">
<p data-start="1006" data-end="1134">Supabase → Database → <strong data-start="1028" data-end="1054">Replication (Realtime)</strong> → enable on <code data-start="1067" data-end="1087">writer_block_posts</code> so new posts show up live on <code data-start="1117" data-end="1133">/writers-block</code>.</p>
</li>
</ul>

problem—I’ve packaged the welcome message so you can drop it in immediately.</p>
<p data-start="81" data-end="214">📦 <strong data-start="84" data-end="127">Download the welcome seed (SQL add-on):</strong><br data-start="127" data-end="130">
<strong data-start="130" data-end="214"><span class="" data-state="closed"><a data-start="132" data-end="212" class="cursor-pointer">chapterhouse-welcome-seed.zip</a></span></strong></p>
<p data-start="216" data-end="230">What’s inside:</p>
<ul data-start="231" data-end="418">
<li data-start="231" data-end="388">
<p data-start="233" data-end="388"><code data-start="233" data-end="260">supabase/welcome_seed.sql</code> → inserts a <strong data-start="273" data-end="285">BFF Host</strong> post from <strong data-start="296" data-end="303">Nia</strong> welcoming <strong data-start="314" data-end="332">TéMonét Damone</strong>, plus two optional follow-ups so the board feels alive.</p>
</li>
<li data-start="389" data-end="418">
<p data-start="391" data-end="418"><code data-start="391" data-end="403">README.txt</code> → tiny how-to.</p>
</li>
</ul>
<h3 data-start="420" data-end="450">How to apply (super quick)</h3>
<ol data-start="451" data-end="790">
<li data-start="451" data-end="554">
<p data-start="454" data-end="554">In Supabase → <strong data-start="468" data-end="482">SQL Editor</strong>, run your main <code data-start="498" data-end="522">supabase/setup_all.sql</code> first (from the big package).</p>
</li>
<li data-start="555" data-end="655">
<p data-start="558" data-end="655">Then open <code data-start="568" data-end="595">supabase/welcome_seed.sql</code> from this ZIP, paste it into the SQL Editor, and <strong data-start="645" data-end="652">Run</strong>.</p>
</li>
<li data-start="656" data-end="740">
<p data-start="659" data-end="740">Make sure Realtime is enabled on <code data-start="692" data-end="712">writer_block_posts</code> (Database → Replication).</p>
</li>
<li data-start="741" data-end="790">
<p data-start="744" data-end="790">Visit <code data-start="750" data-end="766">/writers-block</code> — the welcome is there.</p>
</li>
</ol>

</ul>
<p data-start="99" data-end="240">If you want, I can <strong data-start="118" data-end="169">merge these two into a single updated build ZIP</strong> so you have <strong data-start="182" data-end="204">one master package</strong> for deployment and GitHub import.</p>
<p data-start="242" data-end="372" data-is-last-node="" data-is-only-node="">Do you want me to merge <strong data-start="266" data-end="287">everything inside</strong> both ZIPs while checking for duplicates and keeping the latest version of each file?</p></div></div></div></div><div class="flex min-h-[46px] justify-start"><div class="touch:-me-2 touch:-ms-3.5 -ms-2.5 -me-1 flex flex-wrap data-scroll-anchor="false" data-turn="assistant"><h6 class="sr-only">ChatGPT said:</h6><div class="text-base my-auto mx-auto [--thread-content-margin:--spacing(4)] @[37rem]:[--thread-content-margin:--spacing(6)] @[72rem]:[--thread-content-margin:--spacing(16)] px-(--thread-content-margin)"><div class="[--thread-content-max-width:32rem] @[34rem]:[--thread-content-max-width:40rem] @[64rem]:[--thread-content-max-width:48rem] mx-auto max-w-(--thread-content-max-width) flex-1 group/turn-messages focus-visible:outline-hidden relative flex w-full min-w-0 flex-col agent-turn" tabindex="-1"><div class="flex max-w-full flex-col grow"><div data-message-author-role="assistant" data-message-id="78542078-588b-4189-8dfa-44dc6b7239d3" dir="auto" class="min-h-8 text-message relative flex w-full flex-col items-end gap-2 text-start break-words whitespace-normal [.text-message+&amp;]:mt-5" data-message-model-slug="gpt-5"><div class="flex w-full flex-col gap-1 empty:hidden first:pt-[3px]"><div class="markdown prose dark:prose-invert w-full break-words dark markdown-new-styling"><p data-start="0" data-end="165">You’ll want to <strong data-start="15" data-end="51">start in GitHub repository first</strong>, then open it in Codespaces — here’s the quick step-by-step so you don’t hit the “missing files” problem again:</p>
<hr data-start="167" data-end="170">
<h3 data-start="172" data-end="212"><strong data-start="176" data-end="212">1️⃣ Create the GitHub Repository</strong></h3>
<ol data-start="213" data-end="559">
<li data-start="213" data-end="298">
<p data-start="216" data-end="298">Go to <strong data-start="222" data-end="232">GitHub</strong> → click the green <strong data-start="251" data-end="258">New</strong> button (top left or in your repo list).</p>
</li>
<li data-start="299" data-end="365">
<p data-start="302" data-end="365"><strong data-start="302" data-end="322">Repository name:</strong> <code data-start="323" data-end="341">chapterhouse-app</code> (or whatever you want).</p>
</li>
<li data-start="366" data-end="433">
<p data-start="369" data-end="433"><strong data-start="369" data-end="384">Visibility:</strong> Private (for now, you can make it public later).</p>
</li>
<li data-start="434" data-end="527">
<p data-start="437" data-end="527"><strong data-start="437" data-end="447">Do NOT</strong> add a README, <code data-start="462" data-end="474">.gitignore</code>, or license (we’ll upload everything from your ZIP).</p>
</li>
<li data-start="528" data-end="559">
<p data-start="531" data-end="559">Click <strong data-start="537" data-end="558">Create repository</strong>.</p>
</li>
</ol>
<hr data-start="561" data-end="564">
<h3 data-start="566" data-end="601"><strong data-start="570" data-end="601">2️⃣ Upload Your ZIP Package</strong></h3>
<ol data-start="602" data-end="790">
<li data-start="602" data-end="661">
<p data-start="605" data-end="661">Inside your new repo, click <strong data-start="633" data-end="660">Add file → Upload files</strong>.</p>
</li>
<li data-start="662" data-end="744">
<p data-start="665" data-end="744">Drag and drop the <strong data-start="683" data-end="720"><code data-start="685" data-end="718">chapterhouse-merged-package.zip</code></strong> file into the uploader.</p>
</li>
<li data-start="745" data-end="790">
<p data-start="748" data-end="790">Once uploaded, <strong data-start="763" data-end="789">click "Commit changes"</strong>.</p>
</li>
</ol>
<hr data-start="792" data-end="795">
<h3 data-start="797" data-end="834"><strong data-start="801" data-end="834">3️⃣ Extract Inside Codespaces</strong></h3>
<ol data-start="835" data-end="1272">
<li data-start="835" data-end="931">
<p data-start="838" data-end="931">In your new repo, click the green <strong data-start="872" data-end="880">Code</strong> button → <strong data-start="890" data-end="930">Open with Codespaces → New Codespace</strong>.</p>
</li>
<li data-start="932" data-end="1004">
<p data-start="935" data-end="1004">When Codespaces loads, you’ll see your ZIP file in the file explorer.</p>
</li>
<li data-start="1005" data-end="1088">
<p data-start="1008" data-end="1088"><strong data-start="1008" data-end="1029">Open the terminal</strong> (press &lt;kbd&gt;Ctrl&lt;/kbd&gt; + &lt;kbd&gt;`&lt;/kbd&gt; — the backtick key).</p>
</li>
<li data-start="1089" data-end="1272">
<p data-start="1092" data-end="1096">Run:</p>
<pre class="overflow-visible!" data-start="1100" data-end="1193"><div class="contain-inline-size rounded-2xl relative bg-token-sidebar-surface-primary"><div class="flex items-center <p data-start="1197" data-end="1272">This unpacks your project and removes the ZIP so your root folder is clean.</p>
</li>
</ol>
<hr data-start="1274" data-end="1277">

<p data-start="1425" data-end="1522">Go to <strong data-start="1431" data-end="1444">Ports tab</strong> → <strong data-start="1447" data-end="1472">Make port 3000 public</strong> → click the link to open your app in the browser.</p>
<hr data-start="1524" data-end="1527">
<p data-start="1529" data-end="1740" data-is-last-node="" data-is-only-node="">If you want, I can make you a <strong data-start="1559" data-end="1624">combined “GitHub + Codespaces + First Run” one-page checklist</strong> so you can follow it without switching between chats.<br data-start="1678" data-end="1681">

<h1 data-start="91" data-end="115">Quick fix (60 seconds)</h1>
<ol data-start="116" data-end="571">
<li data-start="116" data-end="236">
<p data-start="119" data-end="182">Make sure the dev server is running:<br data-start="155" data-end="158">
In the terminal, run:</p>

4.28711C7.27713 4.3114 7.38267 4.43665 7.38281 4.58301C7.38281 4.7295 7.27723 4.8546 7.13281 4.87891C6.44249 4.99401 5.94116 5.27746 5.58398 5.66211C5.26908 6.00126 5.05404 6.43267 4.92676 2.0332 4.87891C1.88897 4.85446 1.7832 4.72938 1.7832 4.58301C1.78335 4.43673 1.88902 4.3115 2.0332 4.28711C2.72366 4.17203 3.22481 3.88861 3.58203 3.50391C3.94186 3.11638 4.17214 2.60888 4.28906 2.02441L4.30371 1.97363C4.34801 1.86052 4.45804 1.78333 4.58301 1.7832Z"></path></svg>Edit</button></span></div></div></div><div class="overflow-y-auto p-4" dir="ltr"><code class="whitespace-pre! language-bash"><span><span>npm run dev
</span></span></code></div></div></pre>
<p data-start="219" data-end="236">Leave it running.</p>
</li>
<li data-start="238" data-end="571">
<p data-start="241" data-end="281">Open the <strong data-start="250" data-end="259">Ports</strong> tab (bottom panel).</p>
<ul data-start="285" data-end="571">
<li data-start="285" data-end="335">
<p data-start="287" data-end="335">Find port <strong data-start="297" data-end="305">3000</strong> (or whatever Next printed).</p>
</li>
<li data-start="339" data-end="415">
<p data-start="341" data-end="415">If it shows <strong data-start="353" data-end="364">Private</strong>, click it and set <strong data-start="383" data-end="406">Visibility → Public</strong> again.</p>
</li>
<li data-start="419" data-end="482">
<p data-start="421" data-end="482">Hover the row → click the <strong data-start="447" data-end="456">globe</strong> icon (Open in Browser).</p>
</li>
<li data-start="486" data-end="571">
<p data-start="488" data-end="571">If the globe isn’t there, click the <strong data-start="524" data-end="531">•••</strong> menu on that row → <strong data-start="551" data-end="570">Open in Browser</strong>.</p>
</li>
</ul>
</li>
</ol>
<h1 data-start="573" data-end="602">If the port row isn’t there</h1>
<ul data-start="603" data-end="797">
<li data-start="603" data-end="797">
<p data-start="605" data-end="667">The server might not be listening. Stop it (Ctrl+C), then run:</p>

language-bash"><span><span>npx next dev -H 0.0.0.0 -p 3000
</span></span></code></div></div></pre>
<p data-start="720" data-end="797">This forces Next to bind to the Codespaces interface so the port is detected.</p>
</li>
</ul>
<h1 data-start="799" data-end="841">If it still won’t show “Open in Browser”</h1>
<ul data-start="842" data-end="1020">
<li data-start="842" data-end="950">
<p data-start="844" data-end="950">In the <strong data-start="851" data-end="860">Ports</strong> tab: <strong data-start="866" data-end="884">Forward a Port</strong> → enter <strong data-start="893" data-end="901">3000</strong> → set it to <strong data-start="914" data-end="924">Public</strong> → open via the <strong data-start="940" data-end="949">globe</strong>.</p>
</li>
<li data-start="951" data-end="1020">
<p data-start="953" data-end="1020">Or use the Command Palette: <strong data-start="981" data-end="987">F1</strong> → “Ports: Open Port in Browser”.</p>
</li>
</ul>
<h1 data-start="1022" data-end="1036">Extra checks</h1>
<ul data-start="1037" data-end="1356">
<li data-start="1037" data-end="1143">
<p data-start="1039" data-end="1143">If you switched to <strong data-start="1058" data-end="1068">Public</strong> and it vanished, toggle once: set to <strong data-start="1106" data-end="1117">Private</strong>, then back to <strong data-start="1132" data-end="1142">Public</strong>.</p>
</li>
<li data-start="1144" data-end="1231">
<p data-start="1146" data-end="1231">Disable any pop-up blockers for the codespaces URL; sometimes they block the new tab.</p>
</li>
<li data-start="1232" data-end="1356">
<p data-start="1234" data-end="1356">Grab the URL manually: right-click the port row → <strong data-start="1284" data-end="1306">Copy Local Address</strong> (or <strong data-start="1311" data-end="1328">Copy Port URL</strong>) and paste in your browser.</p>
</li>
</ul>
<p data-start="1358" data-end="1527" data-is-last-node="" data-is-only-node="">Want me to stay on this and walk through what you see on your Ports tab right now? If you paste the status line (visibility, port number), I’ll pinpoint the exact click.</p></div></div></div></div><div class="flex min-h-[46px] justify-start"><div class="touch:-me-2 touch:-ms-3.5 -ms-2.5 -me-1 flex flex-wrap items-center gap-y-4 p-1 select-none touch:w-[calc(100%+--spacing(3.5))] -mt-1 w-[calc(100%+--spacing(2.5))] duration-[1.5s] focus-within:transition-none <hr data-start="569" data-end="572">
<h2 data-start="574" data-end="622"><strong data-start="577" data-end="622">3️⃣ Check if you have path aliases set up</strong></h2>
<ul data-start="623" data-end="758">
<li data-start="623" data-end="758">
<p data-start="625" data-end="758">If <code data-start="628" data-end="632">@/</code> is meant to point to your project root, make sure you have a <code data-start="694" data-end="709">jsconfig.json</code> (or <code data-start="714" data-end="729">tsconfig.json</code>) file in the root like this:</p>
</li>
</ul>
<pre class="overflow-visible!" data-start="759" data-end="861"><div class="contain-inline-size rounded-2xl relative bg-token-sidebar-surface-primary"><div class="flex items-center <ul data-start="862" data-end="934">
<li data-start="862" data-end="934">
<p data-start="864" data-end="934">If you don’t want to use <code data-start="889" data-end="893">@/</code>, just change the import in <code data-start="921" data-end="930">_app.js</code> to:</p>
</li>
</ul>

</span></span></code></div></div></pre>
<p data-start="1042" data-end="1070">Then check <code data-start="1053" data-end="1069">localhost:3000</code>.</p>
<hr data-start="1072" data-end="1075">
<p data-start="1077" data-end="1290">If you want, I can <strong data-start="1096" data-end="1121">update your ZIP build</strong> so the <code data-start="1129" data-end="1149">styles/globals.css</code> file and <code data-start="1159" data-end="1174">jsconfig.json</code> alias setup are already in place before you upload to GitHub/Codespaces — that way this error won’t pop up again.</p>
<p data-start="1292" data-end="1342" data-is-last-node="" data-is-only-node="">Do you want me to prep that fixed ZIP for you now?</p></div></div></div></div><div class="flex min-h-[46px] @[37rem]:[--thread-content-margin:--spacing(6)] @[72rem]:[--thread-content-margin:--spacing(16)] px-(--thread-content-margin)"><div class="[--thread-content-max-width:32rem] @[34rem]:[--thread-content-max-width:40rem] @[64rem]:[--thread-content-max-width:48rem] mx-auto max-w-(--thread-content-max-width) flex-1 group/turn-messages focus-visible:outline-hidden relative flex is looking for a file at <code data-start="57" data-end="79">@/styles/globals.css</code>, but Next.js can’t find it because either:</p>
<ul data-start="126" data-end="238">
<li data-start="126" data-end="181">
<p data-start="128" data-end="181">The <strong data-start="132" data-end="175"><code data-start="134" data-end="154">styles/globals.css</code> file doesn’t exist</strong>, or</p>
</li>
<li data-start="182" data-end="238">
<p data-start="184" data-end="238">The <strong data-start="188" data-end="201"><code data-start="190" data-end="193">@</code> alias</strong> hasn’t been set up in your config.</p>
</li>
</ul>
<hr data-start="240" data-end="243">
<h3 data-start="245" data-end="275"><strong data-start="249" data-end="275">Fix Option 1 — Easiest</strong></h3>
<p data-start="276" data-end="335">Just switch to a relative path so it works without aliases.</p>
<ol data-start="337" data-end="371">
<li data-start="337" data-end="360">
<p data-start="340" data-end="360">Open <code data-start="345" data-end="360">pages/_app.js</code></p>
</li>
<li data-start="361" data-end="371">
<p data-start="364" data-end="371">Change:</p>
</li>
</ol>

<ol start="3" data-start="459" data-end="498">
<li data-start="459" data-end="498">
<p data-start="462" data-end="498">Create the file if it doesn’t exist:</p>
</li>
</ol>
<pre class="overflow-visible!" data-start="499" data-end="551"><div class="contain-inline-size rounded-2xl relative bg-token-sidebar-surface-primary"><div class="flex items-center
